zh.Burslem, Staffordshire, England: 英國英格蘭斯塔福爾布爾斯萊姆
zh.Burslem, Staffordshire, , England: 英國斯塔福爾布爾斯萊姆
zh.Burslem, Stoke-on-Trent district, Staffo: 英國斯塔福爾布爾斯萊姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard William Adams於1478年出生在英國斯塔福爾布爾斯萊姆時，他的父親Richard Adams38歲，母親Margaret Armager38歲。他於大約1489年與Alice Finnyman在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾布爾斯萊姆結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他去世於1530年在他的家鄉享年52歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾布爾斯萊姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard William Adams于1478年出生在英国斯塔福爾布爾斯萊姆时，他的父亲Richard Adams38岁，母亲Margaret Armager38岁。他于大约1489年与Alice Finnyman在英国英格兰斯塔福爾布爾斯萊姆结婚。他们至少是5个儿子的父母。他去世于1530年在他的家乡享年52岁，埋葬在英国英格兰斯塔福爾布爾斯萊姆。

nameMeanings.name: Adams
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Dutch, and German (mainly northwestern Germany): patronymic from the personal name Adam . In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Greek Adamopoulos , Serbian and Croatian Adamović (see Adamovich ), Polish (and Jewish) Adamski .
Irish and Scottish: adopted for McAdam or a Scottish variant of Adam , with excrescent -s.

History: This surname was borne by two early presidents of the US, father and son. They were descended from Henry Adams, who settled in Braintree, MA, in 1635/6, from Barton St. David, Somerset, England. The younger of them, John Quincy Adams (1767–1848) derived his middle name from his maternal grandmother's surname (see   Quincy  ). — Another important New England family, established mainly in NH, is descended from William Adams, who emigrated from Shropshire, England, to Dedham, MA, in 1628. James Hopkins Adams (1812–61), governor of SC, was unconnected with either of these families, his ancestry being Welsh; his forebears entered North America through PA.

variants.name: Adam
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Umberto, Aldo, Giulio, Paolo, Primo, Silvio.
German: Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name Adam , from Adami, the Latin genitive. This surname is also found in France (Lorraine).
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name   Adamo  . This surname is also found in Corsica and southern France.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard William Adams was born in 1478, in Burslem, Staffordshire, England, his father, Richard Adams, was 38 and his mother, Margaret Armager, was 38. He married Alice Finnyman about 1489, in Burslem, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. He died in 1530, in his hometown, at the age of 52, and was buried in Burslem, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard William Adams est né en 1478, à Burslem, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard Adams, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Margaret Armager, avait 38 ans. Il a épousé Alice Finnyman environ 1489, à Burslem, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Il est décédé en 1530, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterré à Burslem, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard William Adams 1478 in Burslem, Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Adams 38 und seine Mutter Margaret Armager 38. Er hat um 1489 in Burslem, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom Alice Finnyman geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne. Er starb 1530 im Alter von 52 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Burslem, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard William Adams è nato il nel 1478 nel Burslem, Staffordshire, England, il suo padre, Richard Adams, aveva 38 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Armager, aveva 38 anni. Lui sposato Alice Finnyman circa 1489, nel Burslem, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli. Morì nel 1530, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolto a Burslem, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard William Adamsが1478年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアブルスレムで生まれた時，父Richard Adamsは38歳，母Margaret Armagerは38歳であった。約1489年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアブルスレムでAlice Finnymanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子の親であった。彼は1530年に故郷で52歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアブルスレムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard William Adams(이)가 1478년 영국 스태퍼드실르 버슬렘에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Adams(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Armager(은)는 38세였다. 그는 대략 1489년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 버슬렘에서 Alice Finnyman(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1530년 그의 고향에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 버슬렘에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard William Adams nasceu em 1478, em Burslem, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Adams, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Armager, tinha 38 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice Finnyman aproximadamente 1489, em Burslem, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1530, em sua cidade natal, com 52 anos, e foi sepultado em Burslem, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard William Adams родился в 1478 г. в местности бурслем, стаффордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Adams было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Armager, 38. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Finnyman около 1489 г. в местности бурслем, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Он умер в 1530 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 52 лет и был похоронен в местности бурслем, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard William Adams nació en 1478, en Burslem, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Adams, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Margaret Armager, tenía 38 años. Se casó con Alice Finnyman aproximadamente 1489, en Burslem, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos. Murió en 1530, en su pueblo, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultado en Burslem, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
